verdict_reasoning: BAX is a pro-apoptotic BCL2 family member canonically localized to the cytosol and mitochondrial outer membrane (MOM). However, during apoptosis BAX undergoes conformational change, oligomerizes, and inserts into the MOM to form pores that release cytochrome c. Additionally, BAX has documented cell-death-induced translocation to the plasma membrane during late apoptosis and secondary necrosis, where it becomes accessible from the extracellular space. This represents a non-baseline surface pool acquired during programmed cell death — qualifying as cell_state_induced surface display.
